{{神社|名称=航空神社|所在地=東京都港区新橋1-18-1 航空会館９階|祭神=航空殉職者6,367柱／航空功労者|創建=1931（昭和6）年11月7日|例祭=9月20日（空の日）}}
'''航空神社'''（こうくうじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]の[[航空会館]]屋上にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 ==
* [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）[[8月1日]] - [[帝国飛行協会]][[理事会]]で航空神社創設を決議。
11月 - [[明治神宮]]造営残木を拝受し、竣工。
[[11月7日]] - 帝国飛行協会飛行館屋上に於いて新総裁[[梨本宮守正王]]参列の下、[[靖国神社]]賀茂[[宮司]]が斎主となり、盛大な[[鎮座|鎮座祭]]を執行。[[祭神]]は航空[[殉職|殉職者]]325柱、霊爾簿作成。
* [[1944年]]（昭和19年）~[[1952年]]（昭和27年） - 神社祭祀中断。
* [[1953年]]（昭和28年） - [[財団法人]][[日本航空協会]]発足により、航空神社の祭祀復活。
* 1963年（昭和38年） - 崇敬者総代会発足、航空功労者を合祀することとなる。 * 同7月 - [[羽田航空神社]]創設に伴う祭神分霊祭を挙行。
* [[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[9月20日]] - 航空神社の神霊を靖国神社境内｢鎮霊社｣に仮奉安のための遷座祭を執行後、仮奉安先の鎮霊社社前で合祀祭および恒例祭挙行。以後1982年まで８年間鎮霊社社前で例祭挙行。祭神、航空殉職者、航空功労者を含め、霊爾簿総数6,367柱
* [[1981年]]（昭和56年）9月 - 靖国神社鎮霊社に於いて航空神社鎮座五十年祭挙行。
* [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 航空神社に関する祭事が、すべて航空神社奉賛会に移る。航空神社の祭祀が従来の祭神慰霊の神社から航空平安祈願祭の神社へと転換。
* [[1983年]]（昭和58年）8月 - 航空神社奉賛会が主導する募金活動により、航空会館屋上に航空神社新社殿落成。
同年8月25日 - 竣工祭並びに遷座祭挙行。
* [[1984年]]（昭和59年） - 毎月第一火曜日を月次祭の日（一月の新年祭は航空協会仕事始めの日）と定め、従来の九月二十日を例大祭の日とし、毎回靖国神社神職の出張奉仕により、毎月航空平安祈願祭を行う、新たな航空神社奉斎方式を定める。
* [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年） - 1月7日の新年祭と9月20日例大祭のみの奉仕となる。
== 関連項目 ==
* [[羽田航空神社]] - [[東京国際空港]]にある分祠。
== 外部リンク ==
* [http://www.aero.or.jp/jinjya/jinjya.html 航空神社] - 日本航空協会公式サイト{{Shinto-stub}}{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}}{{神道 横}}

revision.comment: Nnh がページ「[[航空神社（港区）]]」を「[[航空神社 (東京都港区)]]」に、リダイレクトを残さずに移動しました: 曖昧さ回避の書式誤り。港区は東京都以外にもあるので「東京都」を入れる

{{神社 | 名称=航空神社 | 画像={{画像募集中|cat=東京都港区}} | 所在地=[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]1-18-1航空会館9階 | 祭神=航空殉職者6,367柱／航空功労者 | 神体= | 社格= | 創建=[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）11月7日 | 宮司= | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭=9月20日（空の日） | 神事= }} '''航空神社'''（こうくうじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]の[[航空会館]]屋上にある[[神社]]である。
== 歴史 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）[[8月1日]] - [[帝国飛行協会]][[理事会]]で航空神社創設を決議。 **同年11月 - [[明治神宮]]造営残木を拝受し、竣工。 **[[11月7日]] - 帝国飛行協会飛行館屋上に於いて新総裁[[梨本宮守正王]]参列の下、[[靖国神社]]賀茂[[宮司]]が斎主となり、盛大な[[鎮座|鎮座祭]]を執行。[[祭神]]は航空[[殉職|殉職者]]325柱、霊爾簿作成。 * [[1944年]]（昭和19年）〜[[1952年]]（昭和27年） - 神社祭祀中断。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年） - [[財団法人]][[日本航空協会]]発足により、航空神社の祭祀復活。 * 1963年（昭和38年） - 崇敬者総代会発足、航空功労者を合祀することとなる。 ** 同年7月 - [[羽田航空神社]]創設に伴う祭神分霊祭を挙行。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[9月20日]] - 航空神社の神霊を靖国神社境内｢鎮霊社｣に仮奉安のための遷座祭を執行後、仮奉安先の鎮霊社社前で合祀祭および恒例祭挙行。以後1982年まで８年間鎮霊社社前で例祭挙行。祭神、航空殉職者、航空功労者を含め、霊爾簿総数6,367柱 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）9月 - 靖国神社鎮霊社に於いて航空神社鎮座五十年祭挙行。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 航空神社に関する祭事が、すべて航空神社奉賛会に移る。航空神社の祭祀が従来の祭神慰霊の神社から航空平安祈願祭の神社へと転換。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）8月 - 航空神社奉賛会が主導する募金活動により、航空会館屋上に航空神社新社殿落成。 **同年8月25日 - 竣工祭並びに遷座祭挙行。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年） - 毎月第一火曜日を月次祭の日（一月の新年祭は航空協会仕事始めの日）と定め、従来の9月20日を例大祭の日とし、毎回靖国神社[[神職]]の出張奉仕により、毎月航空平安祈願祭を行う新たな奉斎方式を定める。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年） - 1月7日の新年祭と9月20日例大祭のみの奉仕となる。
== 関連項目 == * [[羽田航空神社]] - [[東京国際空港]]にある分祠。
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.aero.or.jp/jinjya/jinjya.html 航空神社] - 日本航空協会公式サイト{{Shinto-stub}}{{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}}{{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:こうくうしんしや}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:新橋]]

revision.comment: [[:c:Special:Contributions/たてしょ|たてしょ]]さん撮影画像
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=航空神社 | 画像 = [[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
2024年4月撮影 | 所在地=[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]1-18-1航空会館9階 | 祭神=航空殉職者6,367柱／航空功労者 | 神体= | 社格= | 創建=[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）11月7日 | 宮司= | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭=9月20日（空の日） | 神事= }} '''航空神社'''（こうくうじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]の[[航空会館]]屋上に所在する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）[[8月1日]] - [[帝国飛行協会]][[理事会]]で航空神社創設を決議。 **同年11月 - [[明治神宮]]造営残木を拝受し、竣工。 **[[11月7日]] - 帝国飛行協会飛行館屋上に於いて新総裁[[梨本宮守正王]]参列の下、[[靖国神社]]賀茂[[宮司]]が斎主となり、盛大な[[鎮座|鎮座祭]]を執行。[[祭神]]は航空[[殉職|殉職者]]325柱、霊爾簿作成。 * [[1944年]]（昭和19年） - [[1952年]]（昭和27年） - 神社祭祀中断。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年） - [[財団法人]][[日本航空協会]]発足により、航空神社の祭祀復活。 * 1963年（昭和38年） - 崇敬者総代会発足、航空功労者を合祀することとなる。 ** 同年7月 - [[羽田航空神社]]創設に伴う祭神分霊祭を挙行。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[9月20日]] - 航空神社の神霊を靖国神社境内｢鎮霊社｣に仮奉安のための遷座祭を執行後、仮奉安先の鎮霊社社前で合祀祭および恒例祭挙行。以後1982年まで8年間鎮霊社社前で例祭挙行。祭神、航空殉職者、航空功労者を含め、霊爾簿総数6,367柱 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）9月 - 靖国神社鎮霊社に於いて航空神社鎮座五十年祭挙行。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 航空神社に関する祭事が、すべて航空神社奉賛会に移る。航空神社の祭祀が従来の祭神慰霊の神社から航空平安祈願祭の神社へと転換。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）8月 - 航空神社奉賛会が主導する募金活動により、航空会館屋上に航空神社新社殿落成。 **同年8月25日 - 竣工祭並びに遷座祭挙行。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年） - 毎月第一火曜日を月次祭の日（一月の新年祭は航空協会仕事始めの日）と定め、従来の9月20日を例大祭の日とし、毎回靖国神社[[神職]]の出張奉仕により、毎月航空平安祈願祭を行う新たな奉斎方式を定める。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年） - 1月7日の新年祭と9月20日例大祭のみの奉仕となる。 == 関連項目 == * [[羽田航空神社]] - [[東京国際空港]]にある分祠。 * [[航空神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ 航空神社] - 日本航空協会公式サイト {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうくうしんしや}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:新橋]]

revision.comment: Created page with "* {{ill|Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)}} - Located in [[Shimbashi]], [[Minato, Tokyo]], on the rooftop of the Aviation Hall of the {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}."
revision.text: * {{ill|Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)}} - Located in [[Shimbashi]], [[Minato, Tokyo]], on the rooftop of the Aviation Hall of the {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}.

* {{ill|Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)}} - Located in [[Shimbashi]], [[Minato, Tokyo]], on the rooftop of the Aviation Hall of the {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}.
[[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

{{Shrine | Name=Kōkū Shrine | Image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | Location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | Founded=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} '''Kōkū Shrine''' ({{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Kk]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} '''Kōkū Shrine''' ({{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} '''Kōkū Shrine''' ({{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}}) is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ''' November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ''' November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ''' July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ''' August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)-qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)-qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

''' November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ''' November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ''' July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ''' August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)-qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)-qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]]

''' November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ''' November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. ''' July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]

Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[Category:ppp]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}

The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=draft}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=draft}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=draft}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine|12=draft}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|12=draft}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=draft}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮|12=draft}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=draft}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王|12=draft}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|12=draft}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=draft}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=draft}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=draft}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=draft}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=draft}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=draft}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}

Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=draft}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}}

revision.comment: 11 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:航空神社 (東京都港区) up to rev 100517853
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=航空神社 | 画像 = [[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
2024年4月撮影 | 所在地=[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]1-18-1航空会館9階 | 祭神=航空殉職者6,367柱／航空功労者 | 神体= | 社格= | 創建=[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）11月7日 | 宮司= | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭=9月20日（空の日） | 神事= }} '''航空神社'''（こうくうじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]の[[航空会館]]屋上に所在する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）[[8月1日]] - [[帝国飛行協会]][[理事会]]で航空神社創設を決議。 **同年11月 - [[明治神宮]]造営残木を拝受し、竣工。 **[[11月7日]] - 帝国飛行協会飛行館屋上に於いて新総裁[[梨本宮守正王]]参列の下、[[靖国神社]]賀茂[[宮司]]が斎主となり、盛大な[[鎮座|鎮座祭]]を執行。[[祭神]]は航空[[殉職|殉職者]]325柱、霊爾簿作成。 * [[1944年]]（昭和19年） - [[1952年]]（昭和27年） - 神社祭祀中断。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年） - [[財団法人]][[日本航空協会]]発足により、航空神社の祭祀復活。 * 1963年（昭和38年） - 崇敬者総代会発足、航空功労者を合祀することとなる。 ** 同年7月 - [[羽田航空神社]]創設に伴う祭神分霊祭を挙行。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[9月20日]] - 航空神社の神霊を靖国神社境内｢鎮霊社｣に仮奉安のための遷座祭を執行後、仮奉安先の鎮霊社社前で合祀祭および恒例祭挙行。以後1982年まで8年間鎮霊社社前で例祭挙行。祭神、航空殉職者、航空功労者を含め、霊爾簿総数6,367柱 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）9月 - 靖国神社鎮霊社に於いて航空神社鎮座五十年祭挙行。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 航空神社に関する祭事が、すべて航空神社奉賛会に移る。航空神社の祭祀が従来の祭神慰霊の神社から航空平安祈願祭の神社へと転換。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）8月 - 航空神社奉賛会が主導する募金活動により、航空会館屋上に航空神社新社殿落成。 **同年8月25日 - 竣工祭並びに遷座祭挙行。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年） - 毎月第一火曜日を月次祭の日（一月の新年祭は航空協会仕事始めの日）と定め、従来の9月20日を例大祭の日とし、毎回靖国神社[[神職]]の出張奉仕により、毎月航空平安祈願祭を行う新たな奉斎方式を定める。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年） - 1月7日の新年祭と9月20日例大祭のみの奉仕となる。 == 関連項目 == * [[羽田航空神社]] - [[東京国際空港]]にある分祠。 * [[航空神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ 航空神社] - 日本航空協会公式サイト {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうくうしんしや}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:新橋]]

2024年4月撮影 | 所在地=[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]1-18-1航空会館9階 | 祭神=航空殉職者6,367柱／航空功労者 | 神体= | 社格= | 創建=[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）11月7日 | 宮司= | 本殿= | 別名= | 札所等= | 例祭=9月20日（空の日） | 神事= }} '''航空神社'''（こうくうじんじゃ）は、[[東京都]][[港区 (東京都)|港区]][[新橋 (東京都港区)|新橋]]の[[航空会館]]屋上に所在する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == * [[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）[[8月1日]] - [[帝国飛行協会]][[理事会]]で航空神社創設を決議。 **同年11月 - [[明治神宮]]造営残木を拝受し、竣工。 **[[11月7日]] - 帝国飛行協会飛行館屋上に於いて新総裁[[梨本宮守正王]]参列の下、[[靖国神社]]賀茂[[宮司]]が斎主となり、盛大な[[鎮座|鎮座祭]]を執行。[[祭神]]は航空[[殉職|殉職者]]325柱、霊爾簿作成。 * [[1944年]]（昭和19年） - [[1952年]]（昭和27年） - 神社祭祀中断。 * [[1953年]]（昭和28年） - [[財団法人]][[日本航空協会]]発足により、航空神社の祭祀復活。 * 1963年（昭和38年） - 崇敬者総代会発足、航空功労者を合祀することとなる。 ** 同年7月 - [[羽田航空神社]]創設に伴う祭神分霊祭を挙行。 * [[1974年]]（昭和49年）[[9月20日]] - 航空神社の神霊を靖国神社境内｢鎮霊社｣に仮奉安のための遷座祭を執行後、仮奉安先の鎮霊社社前で合祀祭および恒例祭挙行。以後1982年まで8年間鎮霊社社前で例祭挙行。祭神、航空殉職者、航空功労者を含め、霊爾簿総数6,367柱 * [[1981年]]（昭和56年）9月 - 靖国神社鎮霊社に於いて航空神社鎮座五十年祭挙行。 * [[1982年]]（昭和57年） - 航空神社に関する祭事が、すべて航空神社奉賛会に移る。航空神社の祭祀が従来の祭神慰霊の神社から航空平安祈願祭の神社へと転換。 * [[1983年]]（昭和58年）8月 - 航空神社奉賛会が主導する募金活動により、航空会館屋上に航空神社新社殿落成。 **同年8月25日 - 竣工祭並びに遷座祭挙行。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年） - 毎月第一火曜日を月次祭の日（一月の新年祭は航空協会仕事始めの日）と定め、従来の9月20日を例大祭の日とし、毎回靖国神社[[神職]]の出張奉仕により、毎月航空平安祈願祭を行う新たな奉斎方式を定める。 * [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年） - 1月7日の新年祭と9月20日例大祭のみの奉仕となる。 == 関連項目 == * [[羽田航空神社]] - [[東京国際空港]]にある分祠。 * [[航空神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ 航空神社] - 日本航空協会公式サイト {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こうくうしんしや}} [[Category:東京都港区の神社]] [[Category:新橋]]

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)}} {{translated page|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|version=100517853|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location={{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Minato|ja|港区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato|qq=}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōwa|qq=}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Kōkū Shrine|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Minato City|ja|港区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minato City|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|qq=}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|ja|明治神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Shrine|qq=}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|ja|梨本宮守正王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|qq=}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|ja|靖国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasukuni Shrine|qq=}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Tokyo International Airport|ja|東京国際空港|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo International Airport|qq=}}. * {{ill|Kōkū Shrine|ja|航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|version=100517853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]]

Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|version=100517853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shimbashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[ja:%E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[en:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[Category:Shinbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都港区の神社]]

Photographed in April 2024 | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|version=100517853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[sn:E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[Category:Shinbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q126127724]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:航空神社(新橋).jpg|250px]]
Photographed in April 2024 | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]]. == History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Tokyo}} {{Shinto-horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|航空神社 (東京都港区)|version=100517853|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]] [[sn:E8%88%AA%E7%A9%BA%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E6%9D%B1%E4%BA%AC%E9%83%BD%E6%B8%AF%E5%8C%BA)]] [[Category:Shinbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
