<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126127724]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:航空神社(新橋).jpg | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Shinbashi|WD=Q1347299}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.668629|N|139.755844|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|qq=|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|qq=|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126127724]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:航空神社(新橋).jpg | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Shinbashi|WD=Q1347299}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.668629|N|139.755844|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126127724]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:航空神社(新橋).jpg | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Shinbashi|WD=Q1347299}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.668629|N|139.755844|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]][[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Aviation Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)]][[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q126127724]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:20, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:航空神社(新橋).jpg | location=[[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], [[:en:港区|Minato]], {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Shinbashi|WD=Q1347299}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.668629|N|139.755844|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Shimbashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}}, [[:en:港区|Minato City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]].
== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of [[:en:Meiji Shrine|Meiji Shrine]]. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince [[:en:Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa]], with the chief priest from [[:en:Yasukuni Shrine|Yasukuni Shrine]] officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}

Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association

<!--Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)--><!--Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{wikidata link|Q126127724}} [[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at [[:en:Haneda Airport|Tokyo International Airport]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}

Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Kōkū Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:航空神社(新橋).jpg | location={{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato|1=港区}}, {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Q11082979|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}} 1-18-1, 9th floor of the Kōkū Kaikan | Enshrined deity=6,367 spirits of aviation martyrs / contributors to aviation | Sacred object= | Shrine Rank= | consecration_year=November 7, 1931 ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 6) | Chief priest= | Main hall= | Alternate name= | Pilgrimage designation= | Annual festival=September 20 (Sky Day) | Rituals= | prefecture={{ill|Shinbashi|WD=Q1347299|1=Shinbashi}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.668629|N|139.755844|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{ill|Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|ja|航空神社|lt=Aviation Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)|qq=}} is a {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}} shrine located on the rooftop of the {{ill|Kōkū Kaikan|ja|航空会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Kaikan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} in {{ill|Shimbashi|ja|新橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimbashi|1=Q11082979|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11082979}}, {{ill|港区|en|港区|WD=Q14219833|lt=Minato City|1=港区}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}.
== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|en|Meiji Shrine|WD=Q287165|lt=Meiji Shrine|1=Meiji Shrine}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|en|Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|WD=Q339595|lt=Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|1=Prince Nashimoto Morimasa}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} officiating. Initially, 325 aviation martyrs were enshrined, and a spiritual register ({{ill|reiji-bo|ja|霊爾簿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/reiji-bo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) was created. * 1944–1952 - Shrine rituals were suspended. * 1953 - Rituals resumed under the newly established {{ill|Japan Aeronautical Association|ja|日本航空協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan Aeronautical Association|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}}, a public interest foundation. * 1963 - A reverence association was formed, and contributors to aviation were also enshrined. ** July - A separation ritual was conducted for the establishment of {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}. * September 20, 1974 - A transfer ritual was conducted to temporarily house the spirits at the "Chinreisha" ({{ill|Memorial Shrine|ja|鎮霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Memorial Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 靖国神社|1=Yasukuni Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q242803}}) on the grounds of Yasukuni Shrine. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://www.aero.or.jp/shrine/ Kōkū Shrine] - Official website of the Japan Aeronautic Association

<!--Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)--><!--Kōkū Shrine (Minato, Tokyo)--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:航空神社 (東京都港区)]] {{wikidata link|Q126127724}} [[Category: and enshrinement ceremonies were held. * 1984 - Monthly ceremonies were established for the first Tuesday of each month (with the New Year Festival held on the Japan Aeronautic Association's first workday of the year), and September 20 became the date of the annual grand festival. Rituals were performed monthly by dispatched priests from Yasukuni Shrine. * 2002 - Rituals were reduced to the New Year Festival on January 7 and the Grand Festival on September 20.
== Related Sites == * {{ill|Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Aviation Shrine|lt=Haneda Kōkū Shrine|ja|羽田航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haneda Kōkū Shrine|qq=}} - A branch shrine located at {{ill|Haneda Airport|en|Haneda Airport|WD=Q204853|lt=Tokyo International Airport|1=Haneda Airport}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Shinbashi, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Minato, Tokyo]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine shifted its focus from memorial rituals for martyrs to peace prayers for aviation. * August 1983 - With funds raised by the supporters’ association, a new shrine structure was built on the rooftop of the Kōkū Kaikan. ** August 25 - Completion tier 6]]. * {{ill|Aviation Shrines|ja|航空神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine|qq=}}

== History == * August 1, 1931 (Shōwa 6) - The {{ill|Imperial Flying Association|ja|帝国飛行協会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Flying Association|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本航空協会|1=Japan Aeronautic Association|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1770611}} decided to establish Kōkū Shrine during its board meeting. ** November - Construction was completed using leftover materials from the construction of {{ill|Meiji Shrine|en|Meiji Shrine|WD=Q287165|lt=Meiji Shrine|1=Meiji Shrine}}. ** November 7 - The shrine's enshrinement ceremony ({{ill|Chinza|ja|鎮座|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chinza|qq=}}) was held on the rooftop of the Imperial Flying Association Flight Museum, attended by the new president, Prince {{ill|Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|en|Prince Nashimoto Morimasa|WD=Q339595|lt=Nashimoto no Miya Morimasa|1=Prince Nashimoto Morimasa}}, with the chief priest from {{ill|Yasukuni Shrine|en|Yasukuni Shrine|WD=Q242803|lt=Yasukuni Shrine|1=Yasukuni Shrine}} officiating. Subsequent annual festivals were held in front of Chinreisha until 1982. The total number of enshrined spirits, including aviation martyrs and contributors, reached 6,367. * September 1981 - The shrine's 50th anniversary was celebrated at Chinreisha. * 1982 - The shrine’s rituals transitioned to the {{ill|Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja|航空神社奉賛会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōkū Shrine Supporters’ Association|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
